title: Jump Game
description:
Jump Game

You are given an integer array nums. You are initially positioned at the array's first index, and each element in the array represents your maximum jump length at that position.

Return true if you can reach the last index, or false otherwise.

Examples

  • Input: nums = [2,3,1,1,4]
    Output: true
    Explanation: Jump 1 step from index 0 to 1, then 3 steps to the last index.
  • Input: nums = [3,2,1,0,4]
    Output: false
    Explanation: You will always arrive at index 3 no matter what. Its maximum jump length is 0, which makes it impossible to reach the last index.

Constraints

  • 1 <= nums.length <= 10^4
  • 0 <= nums[i] <= 10^5
solution_text:
Jump Game

Algorithm:

  1. Track the furthest index you can currently reach (max_reach)
  2. Iterate through the array; if the current index exceeds max_reach, you can't be here — return false
  3. Otherwise, update max_reach to the max of itself and i + nums[i]
  4. If you finish the loop, the last index was reachable

Time Complexity: O(n) — single pass

Space Complexity: O(1)

Key Insights:

  • Greedy: at each step, only the furthest reachable index matters — you don't need to enumerate paths
  • If any position inside max_reach can extend it past the end, you're done
  • An alternative equivalent formulation scans from the right, tracking a shrinking "goal" index
